PlayerProfile.title: PlayerProfile
PlayerProfile.description: Represents a player's core account information and general statistics within the Axiom Frontier.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PlayerProfile entity.

username.description: The player's chosen display name, unique across the game.

email.description: The player's registered email address, used for communication.

creationDate.description: Timestamp when the player account was created.

lastLoginDate.description: Timestamp of the player's last successful login.

goldBalance.description: The current balance of in-game gold currency held by the player.

axiomCreditBalance.description: The current balance of premium Axiom Credits, often purchased via the storefront.

currentLocationCoordinates.description: A string representing the player's current X,Y,Z coordinates in the game world (e.g., '10.5,20.1,5.0').

currentWorldId.description: Reference to the WorldState entity representing the game world or instance the player is currently in. (Relationship: WorldState 1:N PlayerProfile)

agentIds.description: References to Agent entities owned or controlled by this player. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Agent)

Agent.title: Agent
Agent.description: Represents an autonomous entity within the game world, which can be player-controlled, an NPC, or a monster.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Agent entity.

name.description: The name of the agent, either player-chosen or predefined for NPCs/monsters.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile entity if this agent is player-controlled. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Agent)

npcId.description: Reference to the NPC entity if this agent is an NPC. Null if not an NPC. (Relationship: NPC 1:1 Agent)

health.description: Current health points of the agent.

maxHealth.description: Maximum health points the agent can have.

strength.description: The agent's strength attribute, affecting combat prowess or carrying capacity.

intelligence.description: The agent's intelligence attribute, affecting magic abilities or skill learning.

agility.description: The agent's agility attribute, affecting speed or evasion.

lastActivityUpdate.description: Timestamp of the last update to the agent's activity or state.

positionCoordinates.description: A string representing the agent's current X,Y,Z coordinates in the game world (e.g., '10.5,20.1,5.0').

inventoryItemIds.description: References to Item entities currently in the agent's inventory. (Relationship: Agent N:N Item)

activeQuestIds.description: References to Quest entities currently active for this agent. (Relationship: Agent N:N Quest)

Item.title: Item
Item.description: Represents any purchasable, discoverable, or craftable item within the game world.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Item entity.

name.description: The display name of the item.

description.description: A detailed description of the item's purpose, lore, or effects.

rarity.description: The rarity level of the item, influencing its drop rate or value (e.g., 'common', 'uncommon', 'rare', 'epic', 'legendary').

baseValue.description: The base monetary value of the item in primary in-game currency.

weight.description: The weight of the item, potentially affecting an agent's carrying capacity.

gameAssetId.description: Reference to the GameAsset entity for the primary visual and/or audio representation of this item. (Relationship: Item 1:N GameAsset)

GameAsset.title: GameAsset
GameAsset.description: Manages references to in-game assets and textures stored in Firebase Storage, used for dynamic loading in Godot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GameAsset entity.

name.description: The descriptive name of the asset (e.g., 'SwordModel_V1', 'StoneTexture_HighRes').

storagePath.description: The URI path to the asset file within Firebase Storage.

version.description: The version identifier of the asset, allowing for dynamic updates and caching.

checksum.description: A checksum (e.g., MD5 or SHA256) to verify the integrity of the downloaded asset file.

Quest.title: Quest
Quest.description: Defines dynamically generated or predefined quests that agents can undertake.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Quest entity.

description.description: A detailed description of the quest's storyline, background, and overall goal.

status.description: The current state of the quest (e.g., 'available', 'active', 'completed', 'failed', 'abandoned').

objectives.description: A list of textual objectives that need to be completed for the quest.

rewardItemIds.description: References to Item entities that are awarded upon successful quest completion. (Relationship: Quest N:N Item)

prerequisiteQuestIds.description: References to other Quest entities that must be completed before this quest becomes available. (Relationship: Quest N:N Quest)

requiredAgentType.description: The type of agent required to initiate or complete this quest (e.g., 'any', 'player', 'specific NPC').

isGeneratedByAI.description: Indicates if this quest's content (title, description, objectives) was dynamically generated by an AI.

generationTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the quest was generated or initially made available.

completionTimestamp.description: Timestamp when the quest was completed by an agent. Null if not yet completed.

WorldState.title: WorldState
WorldState.description: Captures the global, persistent state of the game world, updated by the world heartbeat.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorldState entity, typically a fixed ID like 'global_state' or a timestamp for historical records.

civilizationIndex.description: A numerical value representing the overall development, prosperity, and influence of player civilizations.

economyInflationRate.description: The current inflation rate affecting in-game item prices and currency values.

lastHeartbeatTimestamp.description: Timestamp of the last 'world heartbeat' execution, indicating when global logic was last processed.

activeGlobalEventIds.description: References to GlobalEvent entities currently active and influencing the world. (Relationship: WorldState N:N GlobalEvent)

gameTimeElapsedHours.description: Total in-game hours that have elapsed since the world's inception or last reset.

GlobalEvent.title: GlobalEvent
GlobalEvent.description: Defines world-wide events that periodically occur and impact game mechanics or lore.

id.description: Unique identifier for the GlobalEvent entity.

name.description: The name of the global event.

description.description: A detailed description of the event and its impact on the game world and players.

startTime.description: The starting timestamp of the global event.

endTime.description: The ending timestamp of the global event.

effectDescription.description: A summary of the game mechanics affected by this event (e.g., 'Double XP for combat', 'Resource Node Spawn Rate +50%').

Transaction.title: Transaction
Transaction.description: Records financial operations, particularly purchases from the Axiom Storefront, involving real-world or in-game currency.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Transaction entity.

playerId.description: Reference to the PlayerProfile entity who initiated or is involved in the transaction. (Relationship: PlayerProfile 1:N Transaction)

amount.description: The monetary value of the transaction.

currencyUsed.description: The type of currency involved in the transaction (e.g., 'USD', 'EUR', 'AxiomCredit', 'Gold').

itemIdsInvolved.description: References to Item entities involved in this transaction (e.g., items purchased or sold). (Relationship: Transaction N:N Item)

status.description: The current status of the transaction (e.g., 'pending', 'completed', 'failed', 'refunded', 'cancelled').

paymentGatewayTransactionId.description: The external transaction ID provided by the payment gateway (e.g., PayPal).

NPC.title: NPC
NPC.description: Represents Non-Player Characters with unique personalities and roles, potentially AI-generated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the NPC entity.

name.description: The name of the Non-Player Character.

agentId.description: Reference to the associated Agent entity that represents this NPC in the game world, managing its stats and position. (Relationship: NPC 1:1 Agent)

personalityDescription.description: An AI-generated detailed description of the NPC's unique personality and character traits.

dialogueScript.description: A snippet or reference to an extensive AI-generated dialogue script for the NPC, dictating its conversational style.

faction.description: The faction or group to which the NPC belongs, influencing its interactions with other entities.

moodState.description: The NPC's current emotional or behavioral state (e.g., 'friendly', 'hostile', 'neutral', 'curious', 'fearful').

role.description: The primary role of the NPC in the game world (e.g., 'merchant', 'questgiver', 'guard', 'villager', 'craftsman').

definition.description: Contains a player's core account information and general statistics. Authorized by `request.auth.uid == playerId` for read/write. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`playerId`).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player, corresponding to Firebase Authentication UID.

definition.description: Represents an autonomous entity within the game world, specifically owned and controlled by a player. Authorized by `request.auth.uid == playerId` for read/write. The `playerId` from the path is denormalized and the `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`agentId`).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the player's agent.

definition.description: Contains specific instances of items held in a player's agent's inventory, with their own state (e.g., quantity, durability, current condition) and a reference to the global item definition. Authorized by `request.auth.uid == playerId` for read/write. Includes denormalized `playerId` and `agentId` from the path for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the agent whose inventory this item belongs to.

params.name: inventoryItemId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific instance of an item in the agent's inventory.

definition.description: Contains active quests undertaken by a player's agent, tracking specific progress, status, and completion state, with a reference to the global quest definition. Authorized by `request.auth.uid == playerId` for read/write. Includes denormalized `playerId` and `agentId` from the path for authorization independence.

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the owning player.

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier of the agent undertaking this quest.

params.name: activeQuestId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific instance of an active quest for the agent.

definition.description: Records financial operations for a specific player, such as purchases from the Axiom Storefront. Authorized by `request.auth.uid == playerId` for read, system/admin for write. The `playerId` from the path is denormalized and the `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`transactionId`).

params.name: playerId
params.description: The unique identifier of the player involved in the transaction.

params.name: transactionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the transaction.

definition.description: Global definitions for all purchasable, discoverable, or craftable items within the game world. Publicly readable, system/admin writable. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`itemId`).

params.name: itemId
params.description: The unique identifier for the item definition.

definition.description: Global definitions and storage paths for in-game assets and textures used by the Godot client. Publicly readable, system/admin writable. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`gameAssetId`).

params.name: gameAssetId
params.description: The unique identifier for the game asset.

definition.description: Global definitions for dynamically generated or predefined quests (templates). Publicly readable, system/AI/admin writable. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`questId`).

params.name: questId
params.description: The unique identifier for the quest definition.

definition.description: Contains the global, persistent state of the entire game world, typically a singleton document (e.g., 'global_state'). Publicly readable, system (via world heartbeat) and admin writable. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`worldStateId`).

params.name: worldStateId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific world state instance (e.g., 'global_state').

definition.description: Defines world-wide events that periodically occur and impact game mechanics or lore. Publicly readable, system/admin writable. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`globalEventId`).

params.name: globalEventId
params.description: The unique identifier for a global event.

definition.description: Contains definitions for Non-Player Characters (NPCs), including unique personalities and roles. Publicly readable, system/AI/admin writable. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`npcId`).

params.name: npcId
params.description: The unique identifier for the NPC definition.

definition.description: Represents autonomous entities within the game world that are *not* player-controlled (e.g., NPCs, monsters). Publicly readable, system writable. Note: Player-owned agents are segregated under `/playerProfiles/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}`. The `id` field is denormalized as the document ID (`agentId`).

params.name: agentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the non-player-controlled agent.

definition.description: A collection used for Database Access Control (DBAC) to grant administrative privileges. The mere existence of a document in this collection with an `adminId` matching `request.auth.uid` grants admin rights. Authorized for read by `request.auth.uid == adminId`, system writable. This collection enables Authorization Independence for admin checks.

params.name: adminId
params.description: The unique identifier of a user with administrative privileges (Firebase Authentication UID).

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for Axiom Frontier prioritizes Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient query patterns. Authorization independence is primarily achieved through path-based ownership and denormalization. For user-specific data, entities are nested under `/playerProfiles/{playerId}`, making the owning `playerId` directly accessible within security rules from the document path (e.g., `match /playerProfiles/{playerId}/agents/{agentId}` allows rules to check `request.auth.uid == playerId`). This eliminates the need for expensive and non-atomic `get()` operations to check parent document ownership, a critical design principle.

Structural Segregation is applied by separating entities based on their security posture. Global game definitions (e.g., `items`, `gameAssets`, `quests` templates, `npcs` personalities, `globalEvents`, and the `worldState`) are placed in top-level collections, accessible for public reads but restricted to system or admin writes. This clearly delineates static, global content from dynamic, user-specific data. Player-owned game entities like `Agent` (player-controlled), `PlayerInventoryItem` (agent-specific item instances), and `PlayerActiveQuest` (agent-specific quest instances) are strictly nested under the owning player's profile and agent, ensuring that `list` and `get` operations on these collections inherently filter by the authenticated user's ID, thus satisfying QAPs (Queries Are Not Filters). For example, a query to `/playerProfiles/$(request.auth.uid)/agents` will only ever return the authenticated user's agents. NPCs and monsters, which are not player-owned, reside in a separate top-level `/agents` collection to maintain homogeneous security requirements within each collection (public read, system write).

Roles, specifically `admin`, are managed via a dedicated `roles_admin/{adminId}` collection, leveraging the 'existence over content' principle for DBAC. This allows for simple and atomic rule checks (e.g., `exists(/databases/$(database)/documents/roles_admin/$(request.auth.uid))`). All wildcards are descriptive, and consistent naming conventions (`playerId`, `agentId`) are used for clarity and predictability.
